title: Solution for 21607: quadlet RequiresMountsFor breaks on space
body:
Issue: if `Mount` option has a space in the absolute path in quadlet container file, it breaks the `RequiresMountsFor` directive (under the `[Unit]` section and also causes some chaos in the ExecStart), in the transient/generated service.

To solve this issue https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21607
```
--- PASS: Test_Add_OptionWithSpaceValue (0.00s)
PASS
ok      github.com/containers/podman/v5/pkg/systemd/parser      0.003s
--- PASS: Test_Add_OptionWithoutSpaceValue (0.00s)
PASS
ok      github.com/containers/podman/v5/pkg/systemd/parser      (cached)
```

```
[fedora@vps-90b83ef0 quadlet]$ go run . -dryrun --user 2>/dev/null | grep -iE '(RequiresMountsFor|ExecStart)'
...
RequiresMountsFor="/mnt/base part1 part 2"
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name=quadlet-demo-mysql --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups
```

comments.body:

done

```
[fedora@vps-90b83ef0 quadlet]$ go run . -dryrun --user 2>/dev/null | grep -iE '(RequiresMountsFor|ExecStart)'
...
RequiresMountsFor="/mnt/base part1 part 2"
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --mount "type=bind,source=/mnt/base part1 part 2,dst=/mnt" --name=quadlet-demo-mysql --cidfile=%t/%N.cid --replace --rm --cgroups
```

My latest signed commit precludes ExecStart from being quoted in its entirety, only doing so for the `mount` options and other relevant options, whilst catching all else like RequiresMountsFor whose path may not be quoted due to absolute values.

@ygalblum 

I updated the tests for it

I think I wasn't clear. I disagree with @vrothberg and I don't think this should be implemented in the `.Add` method.
There are other cases that do not require quoting (e.g. `ExecStop`, `ExecStopPost` and these are just the examples that come to my mind now). I think the caller to `.Add` needs to know if the key being set is supposed to be quoted or not and handle it before the call.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
What is the status of this is there anything I can help with to get this merged?  From @ygalblum it seems like no additional work is necessary to make this more generic?
